2606.21616.title: LLM and Human Modes of Representation
2606.21616.paper_summary: Much work on the cognitive foundations of AI has focussed on comparisons between the ways in which Large Language Models (LLMs) and humans process information and represent it. One aspect of this comparison involves determining the extent to which LLMs can achieve or surpass human performance on a variety of cognitively interesting tasks. A second explores points of convergence and divergence between LLM and human systems for processing information. Here, I consider some recent research that has addressed both issues in two informational domains. The first is the representation of linguistic knowledge. The second is real world reasoning and planning. While LLMs frequently achieve impressive levels of performance and fluency on linguistic applications, they tend to handle linguistic content in ways that are distinct from human processing. They are also, for the most part, less efficient than humans in learning and generalisation for reasoning tasks.
2606.21616.paper_summary_zh: 許多關於人工智慧認知基礎的研究集中在大型語言模型（LLMs）與人類處理信息和表達信息的方式之間的比較上。這種比較的一個方面涉及確定LLMs在各種認知上有趣的任務中達到或超越人類表現的程度。第二個方面探討了LLM和人類系統在信息處理上的趨同和趨異點。在這裡，我考慮了一些最近的研究，這些研究在兩個信息領域中解決了這兩個問題。第一個是語言知識的表徵。第二個是真實世界的推理和規劃。雖然LLMs在語言應用上經常達到令人印象深刻的表現和流暢度，但它們處理語言內容的方式往往與人類的處理方式不同。在推理任務的學習和概括方面，它們在大多數情況下也不如人類高效。
2606.21616.author: Shalom Lappin et.al.
